steps.title: Enter approximate scatterplot points
steps.content: This is an exponential model with a vertical shift, so use a custom Desmos regression. Reading from the graph, use approximate points such as $(-4,19)$, $(-3,19)$, $(-2,20)$, $(-1,22)$, $(0,24)$, $(1.7,30)$, $(3,42)$, $(4,50)$, and $(4.3,57)$.

steps.title: Run shifted exponential regression
steps.content: Use the custom regression $y_1 \sim a b^{x_1}+c$. The fitted curve has a horizontal level near $18$, growth base near $1.55$, and coefficient near $6$, matching $y=6(1.55)^x+18$.

steps.title: Confirm against the choices
steps.content: Choice A has no vertical shift, so it is too low on the left side. Choice B has intercept $42$, too high at $x=0$. Choice C has the right low-end level but grows far too slowly; at $x=3$ it is only about $26.7$. Choice D is near $40$ at $x=3$ and follows the plotted curve. Choice D is correct.
